scenarios.name: Scenario 1: Clear Grid
scenarios.description: All junctions have LOW traffic. The shortest route A -> B -> C -> F -> I should be chosen.

scenarios.name: Scenario 2: Congested Shortest Path
scenarios.description: Junction B and C have HIGH traffic. The optimizer should bypass them and route through the lower part: A -> D -> G -> H -> I.

scenarios.name: Scenario 3: Western Congestion
scenarios.description: Junction D and G have HIGH traffic. The optimizer should choose the upper route: A -> B -> C -> F -> I.

scenarios.name: Scenario 4: Diagonal Blockage
scenarios.description: Junction B and E have HIGH traffic. The optimizer must choose A -> D -> G -> H -> I to reach the hospital.

scenarios.name: Scenario 5: Mixed Traffic Gridlock
scenarios.description: Heavy traffic blocks both normal shortest routes (B is HIGH, D is MEDIUM, E is HIGH). The router should select A -> D -> G -> H -> I as the most optimal path.
